Fame.text: Your Fame total at the end of the game — the base of your score, before any Achievements or scenario bonuses.

Greatest Knowledge.text: 2 Fame per Spell in your deck, plus 1 Fame per Advanced Action in your deck.

Greatest Leader.text: The total level of all your Units. A wounded Unit only counts half its level, rounded down.

Greatest Adventurer.text: 2 Fame per Shield token you placed on an adventure site (dungeons, tombs, monster dens, ziggurats/pyramids, etc.).

Greatest Loot.text: 2 Fame per Artifact (in your deck or on a Unit), plus 1 Fame for every 2 crystals in your Inventory.

Greatest Conqueror.text: 2 Fame per Shield token you placed on a keep, mage tower, or monastery.

Greatest Beating.text: Minus 2 Fame per Wound card in your deck. Wounds attached to a Unit don't count.

Greatest Quester.text: 1 Fame per Quest Point you scored from completed Quest card steps (Apocalypse Dragon expansion).
Greatest Quester.source: Apocalypse Dragon Variant Rules, rulebook p.43

Cities Conquered.text: +10 Fame for each city you conquered this game.

All Cities Conquered.text: +15 Fame bonus if you conquered every city in the game — this is also what determines whether you Won or Lost.

Rounds Finished Early.text: +30 Fame for each Round you finished before the 6-Round limit — i.e. you beat the clock.

Dummy Player's Deck.text: +1 Fame for each card still unflipped in the Dummy Player's Deed deck at game end — a fuller deck means you finished with time to spare.

End of Round.text: +5 Fame if "End of the Round" had not yet been announced during your final Round.

City Revealed.text: +10 Fame if you revealed the map's capital city by the end of the game — this is also what determines whether you Won or Lost.
City Revealed.source: First Reconnaissance scoring, Solo, Apocalypse Dragon rulebook p.23

High Priestess Defeated.text: +20 Fame if you defeated the High Priestess — this is also what determines whether you Won or Lost.
High Priestess Defeated.source: The Hidden Valley scoring, Solo, Shades of Tezla Scenario Book p.26

Graveyards Sealed.text: +5 Fame for each Graveyard you sealed, plus +10 more if you sealed both Graveyards and also defeated the Necromancer.
Graveyards Sealed.source: The Realm of the Dead scoring, Solo, Shades of Tezla Scenario Book p.25

Necromancer Defeated.text: +10 Fame if you defeated the Necromancer — sealing both Graveyards and defeating the Necromancer is also what determines whether you Won or Lost.
Necromancer Defeated.source: The Realm of the Dead scoring, Solo, Shades of Tezla Scenario Book p.25

For the Council: Quest Points.text: Your total Quest Points scored — the base of your score in this Quest-driven scenario. There's no Fame or Standard Achievements scoring in For the Council.
For the Council: Quest Points.source: For the Council scoring, Solo, Apocalypse Dragon rulebook p.25

For the Council: Reputation.text: Tap the space your Shield token is actually on. You score (or lose) Quest Points equal to the modifier printed there — or −10 instead on an X space. Reputation +2 or higher (2 spaces past center) determines whether you Won or Lost.
For the Council: Reputation.source: For the Council scoring, Solo, Apocalypse Dragon rulebook p.25

Round-Prep Offers.text: When a Dummy Player is in play, round-prep changes: the lowest Advanced Action offer card goes into the Dummy Player's deck (which is then reshuffled) instead of being discarded, and the lowest Spell offer card still goes to the bottom of the Spell deck as usual but also grants the Dummy Player one crystal of its color, with no 3-crystal cap.
Round-Prep Offers.source: Cooperative and Solo Scenarios, Dummy Player, rulebook p.15
